mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >70% of cases, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline occurs but 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances, or interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output that supports short-term equity momentum and generates positive weekly returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of cases during recovery regimes, or predictive power vanishes outside recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210, inflation expectations accelerate and force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL crosses 210 but S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or threshold loses predictive edge in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and supports corporate revenue projections, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases but 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing real GDP growth and rising consumer prices creates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration coincides with CPI acceleration but S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth alongside elevated federal funds rates indicates a resilient economy that absorbs tight monetary policy, generating positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: High rates and strong production occur but 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform baseline in walk-forward tests.
